accept: Reviewer agent returns a structured findings report (severity-grouped, file:line refs, suggested fixes).
Each non-duplicate finding is filed as its own act issue with appropriate priority and type.
Duplicates of already-filed issues are noted and not re-filed.
This issue closes with a list of the derivative issues created (in close --reason).

Lessons about review process itself (what worked / didn't in this first pass) are captured in a CLAUDE.md update or filed as a separate process-improvement issue, so the second review is better than the first.
payload.description:
First overall code review of act since the dispatcher pipeline shipped v0.1.0. Triggered 2026-05-10 by Andrew during the dogfood session. The codebase has had ~10 follow-up issues land since v0.1.0; a structural review before we add many more is the right gate.

Reviewer: feature-dev:code-reviewer (in flight at file time). Scope: bugs, logic errors, security, public-surface inconsistencies, doc-vs-behavior drift, test coverage gaps. Confidence-filtered to >70% so we get signal not noise. Findings will be filed as derivative act issues, then this issue closes with a pointer to them.

Future: this is the seed of a recurring review pattern (different reviewers, different focuses, both pre-implementation plan reviews and post-implementation solution reviews). Likely lives as a harness-level slash command that files+runs reviews; the review-as-act-task pattern is the audit trail that makes that scale.
payload.title: overall codebase review of act at v0.1.0+
